repo.name: ml-learning-path
repo.description: A structured, hands-on ML learning path from statistics fundamentals to applied algorithms — Python, Scikit-learn, and Google Colab.

initial_files.content:
numpy
pandas
matplotlib
seaborn
scikit-learn
jupyter

initial_files.note: Use GitHub's Python .gitignore template — will be auto-generated if gitignore_template is set

gh_cli_commands.create_repo: gh repo create ml-learning-path --public --description "A structured, hands-on ML learning path from statistics fundamentals to applied algorithms — Python, Scikit-learn, and Google Colab." --gitignore Python --license mit
gh_cli_commands.add_topics: gh repo edit ml-learning-path --add-topic machine-learning --add-topic python --add-topic scikit-learn --add-topic data-science --add-topic jupyter-notebook --add-topic google-colab --add-topic beginner-friendly --add-topic learning-path
gh_cli_commands.push_readme: git init && git add README.md requirements.txt && git commit -m 'Initial commit: README and repo setup' && git branch -M main && git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/ml-learning-path.git && git push -u origin main

notes.replace: Replace YOUR_USERNAME in gh_cli_commands.push_readme with your actual GitHub username before running.
notes.colab_links: Colab notebook links are embedded in the .pptx slide files. Extract and add them to notebooks/ as .ipynb files or link them in resources/AI_ML_Links.md.
